2 — Market Intelligence
2.1 Market Situation
NE (NJ)
Overall Market TrendGrowing demand for OKF Aloe, POCAS beverages and Josh Bosh Jelly at NE C-store and ethnic supermarkets.
Customer Demand ShiftShift toward health-functional drinks. POCAS Sunrise Smoothie gaining traction in NJ health channel.
Regional DifferencesNY/NJ dense Asian community — steady POCAS/OKF loyalty. MD/VA growing Popping Boba demand.
Pricing PressureStable. Moderate competition from UNFI-distributed Korean brands.
SE (FL)
Overall Market TrendFL market expanding for tropical and health beverages. Caribbean grocery channel growing.
Customer Demand ShiftFL C-store and Caribbean export demand for Sunrise Smoothie and OKF Aloe increasing.
Regional DifferencesSouth FL diverse demographic drives demand for tropical and Caribbean-inspired drinks.
Pricing PressurePrice competition from local FL beverage brands. El Mejor recovery is pricing priority.
SE (GA)
Overall Market TrendSteady growth in GA C-store. FL market developing alongside OKF and POCAS tropical SKUs.
Customer Demand ShiftGA C-store and Latin grocery active. FL gaining interest in Sunrise Smoothie and Josh Bosh Jelly.
Regional DifferencesAtlanta urban markets demand premium Asian beverages. GA wholesale pricing competitive.
Pricing PressurePrice-sensitive GA C-store channel. Standard margin maintained at Diaz Foods.
SW (TX)
Overall Market TrendStrong TX C-store growth. TOK TOK +120% YoY. CO and AZ developing new territory.
Customer Demand ShiftTOK TOK and Josh Bosh Jelly strong in TX C-store. Emerging demand for new POCAS SKUs.
Regional DifferencesTX C-store dominant channel. CO/AZ newer markets with growing Asian beverage awareness.
Pricing PressureCompetitive pricing from Mexican beverage brands. POCAS positioned as premium Asian alternative.
MW (IL)
Overall Market TrendStable IL wholesale base. New accounts in KS, WI, IN expanding MW footprint significantly.
Customer Demand ShiftDemand growing for Josh Bosh Jelly Jars and Mochi Bags at Valu Merchandisers and Banner.
Regional DifferencesIL metro accounts strong. KS/WI rural accounts newer to POCAS/OKF brand awareness.
Pricing PressurePricing stable. POCAS positioned at premium tier vs lower-priced Chinese imports.
WC (CA)
Overall Market TrendCA market recovering post-Pitco loss. NorCal natural channel and SoCal C-store developing.
Customer Demand ShiftHealth consumers driving Sunrise Smoothie and MushHood at CA natural food stores.
Regional DifferencesSoCal Asian grocery and C-store strong for OKF. NorCal natural food channel for POCAS.
Pricing PressureCA distributor pricing under pressure. Grocery Outlet volume growing at competitive price points.
NAT
Overall Market TrendNational chain retail on track. TJX HomeGoods and Dollar General stable. E-commerce growing.
Customer Demand ShiftChain retail demand for giftable, unique Asian food and beverage items increasing.
Regional DifferencesRegional variation: ethnic grocery/C-store regional focus vs. national chain retail.
Pricing PressureChain retail pricing fixed. Independent accounts offer better margin flexibility.

NE (NJ)Growing — Josh Bosh Jelly Animal Jars (Cow, Doggy, Piggy) launched Jan. Costa Rica Jelly active. High retailer interest.Amos Jelly dominant at Jetro & C&C. Price gap: Amos ~$18/cs vs Josh Bosh ~$22/cs. Differentiate on novelty format.NY Produce account recovered after Jan container arrival. No shortage. New SKUs landed on time.Push Josh Bosh Jelly Jars to 5+ new wholesale accounts. Use novelty animal format as differentiation vs Amos.
SE (GA)Moderate — Josh Bosh Jars new to SE market. FL conv. store channel starting to sample.Amos & Prince of Peace ginger candy competing for shelf at GA supermarkets.Adequate stock. New items arriving. No internal shortage.Present Josh Bosh SKUs to K-VA-T and Food Lion GA. Target FL conv. distributors.
SE (FL)Moderate — Josh Bosh Jelly Jars new to FL C-store. Caribbean grocery accounts showing early interest.Amos and local Mexican candy brands competing at FL C-stores. Price gap $2–3/cs vs Josh Bosh.Adequate stock. FL distribution coverage limited — new SKUs not yet placed at El Mejor.Sample Josh Bosh Jelly Animal Jars to FL C-store distributors (El Mejor, Navarro). Target Caribbean grocery.
SW (TX)Growing — TOK TOK +120% in Jan. Josh Bosh Jelly Jars selling through TX C-store distributors.Amos competitive in C-store channel. Local Mexican candy brands at lower price points.Adequate inventory. Jelly/candy category growing fast in TX.Bundle Jelly Jars with Bubble Tea offers to key TX C-store distributors.
MW (IL)Strong launch — Josh Bosh Animal Jars all 5 SKUs ranked top 20 POCAS items. 150–190 cs/SKU in Jan.Chinese-origin jelly brands at lower price competing at Valu Merchandisers.New SKUs landed Jan 2026 — no shortage. Monitor reorder pace.Strong sell-in at 23 wholesale accounts. Track March repeat order rate closely.
WC (CA)Moderate — Costa Rica Jellies 725 cases ($15,109) in first distribution months.Local Mexican candy brands competitive in SoCal. Korean jelly snacks in Asian grocery channel.N/A — Adequate stock for current demand.Build Costa Rica Jelly distribution in SoCal ethnic grocery channel via Julio accounts.
